source.excerpt: declared an interim ordinary dividend of 19.0 pence per share

giving a total ordinary dividend for the year of 69.0 pence per share

with an overall engagement score of 72% in the most recent colleague engagement opinion survey.

business_facts.statement: Greggs' purpose is to make great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink accessible to everyone, with a vision to be the customers' favourite for food-on-the-go.

source.excerpt: To make great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink accessible to everyone

business_facts.statement: Greggs is rated No.1 for value on the YouGov BrandIndex 2025 within the quick-service restaurant, coffee shop and delivery services group.

source.excerpt: Greggs is rated No.1 for value on the YouGov BrandIndex 2025*, within the quick-service restaurant, coffee shop and delivery services group.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates a 2,700-strong shop estate and has expanded grocery and delivery partnerships.

business_facts.statement: Greggs has over 33,000 colleagues across its business in shops, supply chain and central support teams.

source.excerpt: We have over 33,000 amazing colleagues across our business

risk_disclosures.description: Cyber security and systems change are risks requiring management focus, particularly given well-publicised issues faced by other retailers. Technological change continues to accelerate.

source.excerpt: Cyber security and our plans to manage the business during a period of significant systems change were both in focus, particularly given the well-publicised issues faced by other retailers. Technological change continues to accelerate

risk_disclosures.description: Allergen management is a material risk area for a food business. The Board receives updates on progress and measures being taken to protect customers.

source.excerpt: As a food business, our processes for allergen management continued to be an area of Board focus, given the material risk associated with this.

risk_disclosures.description: Consumer sentiment is expected to remain a headwind in 2026, presenting a market challenge despite the company's competitive position.

source.excerpt: We expect consumer sentiment to continue to be a headwind in 2026

strategic_items.description: Five core strategic pillars: (1) Broadening customer appeal and driving loyalty; (2) Growing and developing the Greggs estate; (3) Developing digital channels; (4) Expanding evening trade; (5) Investing in supply chain and technology.

source.excerpt: focus on our core strategic pillars… 1. Broadening customer appeal and driving loyalty 2. Growing and developing the Greggs estate 3. Developing our digital channels 4. Expanding our evening trade 5. Investing in our supply chain and technology for a bigger business

strategic_items.description: Development and enhancement of digital channels through Click + Collect and the Greggs App to offer more value and convenience to customers, with the App enabling direct customer communication and loyalty rewards.

source.excerpt: we have also been developing and enhancing our digital channels to offer more value and convenience to our customers via Click + Collect and the Greggs App.

strategic_items.description: Expansion of the shop estate through growing and developing locations while also expanding partnerships in grocery and delivery to broaden customer reach.

source.excerpt: we have been working to expand and improve our 2,700-strong shop estate, as well as our grocery and delivery partnerships.

strategic_items.description: Investment plans in infrastructure and systems are progressing well and will provide the capability needed to realise significant growth opportunity ahead.

source.excerpt: Our investment plans are progressing well and will provide the infrastructure with which to realise the significant growth opportunity that lies ahead.

management_commentary.statement: Greggs outperformed a tough market in 2025 and delivered continued strategic progress, demonstrating its resilience. The company increased market share and maintained competitiveness despite headwinds in the food-to-go market.

source.excerpt: Greggs outperformed a tough market in 2025 and delivered the continued strategic progress that will support further growth in the business, demonstrating its resilience.

management_commentary.statement: Management is executing investment plans in systems and supply chain capacity that will help realise the company's growth ambition in the years to come.

source.excerpt: We also made good progress investing in the systems and supply chain capacity that will help us to realise our ambition in the years to come.

management_commentary.statement: The Board expects consumer sentiment to continue to be a headwind in 2026, but with a strong competitive position and clear growth opportunities, Greggs can weather these conditions and continue to outperform the market.

source.excerpt: We expect consumer sentiment to continue to be a headwind in 2026, but with a strong competitive position and a clear opportunity for further growth Greggs can weather these conditions and continue to outperform the market.

management_commentary.statement: The Board remains confident in the prospects for further profitable growth over the medium term, with a strong brand and financial position.

source.excerpt: Our brand and financial position remain strong and the Board remains confident in the prospects for further profitable growth over the medium term.

source.excerpt: we will continue to focus on this through strong cost control and structural efficiency opportunities

source.excerpt: we are successfully increasing access to Greggs through the extension of our own shop estate alongside partnerships with grocery, franchise and delivery partners

source.excerpt: We expect this will be a developing trend and are confident in our ability to evolve our range to appeal to those looking for different nutritional profiles and portion sizes when eating out of home

management_commentary.statement: We remain confident that demand for convenient food-on-the-go as customers go about their busy lives will continue to underpin the market. In the year ahead we expect market conditions will remain challenging for the consumer.

source.excerpt: We remain confident that demand for convenient food-on-the-go as customers go about their busy lives will continue to underpin the market. In the year ahead we expect market conditions will remain challenging for the consumer.

business_facts.statement: Greggs is ranked number four in the market for dinner visits with evening sales representing 9.4% of company-managed shop sales

business_facts.statement: The Greggs App was used by over 1.5 million customers a week at peak in December 2025, up from over 500,000 in December 2022

source.excerpt: At the peak in December 2025, the App was used by over 1.5 million customers a week – over three times more than the December peak in 2022.

source.excerpt: scanned in more than a quarter of company-managed transactions (26.7%, up from 20.1% in the previous year)

business_facts.statement: Greggs products are now available at 800 larger Tesco stores and online, with further expansion to 1,900 Tesco Express stores from January 2026

source.excerpt: Greggs products are now available at 800 larger Tesco stores and online. From January 2026 a subset of this range became available in a further 1,900 Tesco Express stores.

business_facts.statement: Greggs employs more than 33,000 people across the UK, adding over 1,200 colleagues in 2025 through 121 net new shop openings

source.excerpt: We are proud to employ more than 33,000 people across the UK in stable, fairly-paid jobs. In a wider environment of rising unemployment, we are proud to be creating new jobs; in opening 121 net new shops we added over 1,200 colleagues to our team.

business_facts.statement: New National Distribution Centres in Derby and Kettering are on track, with Derby ready for robotic picking from mid-2026 and full operation by end of 2026

source.excerpt: Our plans to open new National Distribution Centres in Derby and Kettering remain on time and are both on track to come in under budget. At the Derby site, we will be ready to roll out upstream robotic picking of frozen goods from mid-2026. The 23-acre site will be fully operational by the end of 2026

risk_disclosures.description: Risk that new shop openings could cannibalize sales from existing shops; mitigated by monitoring customer behaviour via Greggs App analysis

source.excerpt: As we grow our estate, we continually monitor customer behaviour to ensure that new openings are not at risk of cannibalising existing shop sales. Analysis of our Greggs App customers continues to demonstrate that those who visit a new

risk_disclosures.description: Consumer environment is expected to remain tough in 2026, though management is optimistic that inflationary pressure will ease

source.excerpt: We expect that 2026 will be another tough year for the consumer but are optimistic that inflationary pressure will ease a little, providing some support to consumers and the food-to-go sector.

risk_disclosures.description: Profit progress in 2026 will be impacted by increased fixed costs from commissioning the new Derby distribution centre, primarily in the second half

source.excerpt: We will see an increase in fixed costs as we commission the new Derby site, which will primarily impact the second half.

strategic_items.description: Expansion into alternative sites such as petrol forecourts, supermarkets, retail parks, hospitals, university campuses, transport hubs, and roadsides to increase customer access beyond traditional high street locations

source.excerpt: with over half of our new openings located in alternative sites such as petrol forecourts, supermarkets, retail parks, hospitals and university campuses. We remain focused on expanding our presence in major transport hubs

strategic_items.description: Relocation of existing shops to better locations to add space and serve more customers whilst retaining the existing shop team

source.excerpt: Relocating shops enables us to retain the existing shop team whilst adding the space needed to serve more customers and expand our offer in that community.

strategic_items.description: Investment in new National Distribution Centres in Derby and Kettering with automation and production capacity to improve logistics efficiency

source.excerpt: Our plans to open new National Distribution Centres in Derby and Kettering remain on time and are both on track to come in under budget. The 23-acre site will be fully operational by the end of 2026, including our first production line on the site, adding capacity to both our manufacturing and logistics operations.

strategic_items.description: Development of unattended retail solutions to serve additional customer missions and enhance returns

source.excerpt: In addition to the bitesize trial, we are developing unattended retail solutions to serve additional missions and further enhance returns.

strategic_items.description: Expanding evening daypart offer with hot, filling meal options and delivery-optimized products to capitalize on fastest-growing daypart opportunity

source.excerpt: we're strengthening our offer for sit-in and delivery occasions by providing hot, filling meals. Products like Southern Fried Chicken Goujons, Southern Fried Potato Wedges and Mozzarella & Cheddar Bites are key drivers of like-for-like volume growth in the evenings

strategic_items.description: Increasing adoption of renewable HVO biofuel across logistics operations, with expansion planned at Leeds and Kettering sites in 2026

source.excerpt: Building on this progress, we are exploring opportunities to expand HVO usage across our Leeds and Kettering logistics sites in 2026, aiming, where viable, to support increased adoption and further reduce emissions.

strategic_items.description: Leveraging digital capabilities including AI-driven CRM, real-time data analytics, and decision-support tools to improve operations and customer service

source.excerpt: At our Head Office our support teams are benefiting from the investment in CRM capability, with AI functionality being developed to drive service standards and efficiencies. These enhancements continue to strengthen our data and AI capabilities and improve our ability to run efficient, well-informed operations.

strategic_items.description: Continued evolution of Greggs App loyalty proposition with personalized product promotion, gamification (Quests feature), and expansion of engagement opportunities to increase customer frequency

source.excerpt: In May, we introduced a personalised inbox, Baked for You, to the Greggs App, giving us more space to promote new products and deals. In November, we launched Greggs Quests to 'gamify' the App user experience and provide more opportunities to drive engagement and frequency of purchase

strategic_items.description: Three core strategic pillars for next five years: building stronger, healthier communities; making our planet safer; and striving to be a better business

source.excerpt: Our priorities for the next five years are based around the same three pillars: building stronger, healthier communities; making our planet safer; and striving to be a better business.

strategic_items.description: Climate commitments: Scope 2 net zero by 2030 and full net zero across Scopes 1-3 by 2040

source.excerpt: maintaining our climate ambition to reach Scope 2 net zero by 2030, and stay firmly on track for full net zero across Scopes 1–3 by 2040

management_commentary.statement: Increasing the frequency of customer visits remains a clear opportunity when compared with best in class for food-to-go

source.excerpt: Despite Greggs success over many years, increasing the frequency of customer visits remains a clear opportunity when compared with best in class for food-to-go.

management_commentary.statement: Managing costs closely is, and always has been, strategically important to us as a value retailer

source.excerpt: Managing costs closely is, and always has been, strategically important to us as a value retailer.

management_commentary.statement: We expect that 2026 will be another tough year for the consumer but are optimistic that inflationary pressure will ease a little

source.excerpt: We expect that 2026 will be another tough year for the consumer but are optimistic that inflationary pressure will ease a little, providing some support to consumers and the food-to-go sector.

management_commentary.statement: Management's expectations for 2026 profit before tax expected to be broadly in line with 2025, with any year-on-year improvement contingent on a recovery in the consumer backdrop

source.excerpt: management's expectations for the year remain unchanged, with forward look profit before tax expected to be broadly in line with 2025 and any year-on-year improvement contingent on a recovery in the consumer backdrop.

management_commentary.statement: The key focus for management in the coming years will be restoring the Company's return on capital employed back to our target of around 20%

source.excerpt: The key focus for management in the coming years will be restoring the Company's return on capital employed ('ROCE') back to our target of around 20%.
Extending our lead to achieve 37.4 points

source.excerpt: we continued to be the UK's leading food-to-go brand based on YouGov BrandIndex scores, ending the year as the No.1 brand in 7 out of 16 YouGov categories

strategic_items.description: Greggs has a strong pipeline of new shop openings and significant opportunity to enhance the estate through strategic relocations and modern refits, with investment in supply chain to support up to 3,500 shops across the UK.

source.excerpt: We have a strong pipeline of new shop openings and a significant opportunity to enhance our estate through strategic relocations and modern refits. We're investing in our supply chain to build the capacity required to support up to 3,500 shops across the UK

strategic_items.description: Developing digital channels through delivery partnerships with Just Eat and Uber Eats to extend reach beyond passing footfall, and Click + Collect functionality for customer convenience.

source.excerpt: Delivery partnerships with Just Eat and Uber Eats extend our reach beyond passing footfall, driving higher-value orders by serving multiple customers at once. Meanwhile, Click + Collect lets customers browse the menu, customise their order, and skip the queue

strategic_items.description: Expanding evening trade through extended trading hours, new menu additions, and leveraging walk-in and digital channels to grow food-to-go sales in the evening market.

source.excerpt: By offering extended trading hours in many of our shops, introducing exciting new menu additions and leveraging both walk-in and digital channels, we have a clear strategic opportunity to grow food-to-go sales in the evening market

strategic_items.description: Core strategic pillars focused on great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink; best customer experience with fast, friendly service; ownership of supply chain for quality and value; and first-class support teams with investments in systems and technology.

source.excerpt: Great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink. Nothing beats freshly baked, freshly prepared food. With bold flavours, responsibly sourced ingredients and consistent quality at outstanding value, Greggs sets the standard for food-on-the-go.

management_commentary.statement: Following a challenging year for food-to-go brands, Greggs continued to be the UK's leading food-to-go brand. With consumer spending continuing to come under pressure, Greggs remained the No.1 brand for value.

source.excerpt: Following a challenging year for food-to-go brands, we continued to be the UK's leading food-to-go brand. With consumer spending continuing to come under pressure, we remained the No.1 brand for value

management_commentary.statement: During 2025 Greggs developed data-driven and digital workstreams with focus on how data and AI can inform strategy, including improving customer segmentation for loyalty scheme participants and introducing new App features such as Greggs Quests.

source.excerpt: During 2025 we continued to develop a range of data-driven and digital workstreams, with a focus on how data and AI can inform and shape our wider strategy. This includes improving how we segment customers who participate in our loyalty scheme

management_commentary.statement: Although the UK Government delayed new regulations on Less Healthy Food until January 2026, Greggs adhered to the spirit of the legislation from its planned introduction date of October 2025, adapting its paid media strategy and content across relevant channels.

source.excerpt: Although the UK Government delayed the new advertising regulations on Less Healthy Food until January 2026, we adhered to the spirit of the legislation for its planned introduction date of October 2025, adapting our paid media strategy and content across relevant channels

ending the year with more than 2,700 Greggs shops across the UK

financial_figures.label: New Tesco stores with Bake at Home products

source.excerpt: Our core range of Bake at Home Greggs products is now available at 800 Tesco Extra stores and online.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates over 2,700 shops across the UK and is expanding toward an ambition of more than 3,500 shops in the longer term. Over half of shops are now in alternative locations beyond the high street.

source.excerpt: we opened 121 net new shops during 2025, ending the year with more than 2,700 Greggs shops across the UK. We continue to focus on broadening our presence beyond the high street and over half of our shops are now in alternative locations.

business_facts.statement: Greggs has expanded its presence in Northern Ireland to 28 shops by 2025 and operates in grocery partnerships including Bake at Home ranges in Iceland supermarkets and a new Tesco partnership introduced in September 2025 covering 800 Tesco Extra stores.

source.excerpt: We continued our expansion in Northern Ireland, opening eight new shops in 2025 taking our total there to 28. We launched a new partnership with Tesco in September 2025. Our core range of Bake at Home Greggs products is now available at 800 Tesco Extra stores and online.

business_facts.statement: Greggs trialled a new bitesize shop format in three high-footfall locations to enter smaller, previously unreachable locations such as travel hubs. Three bitesize shops opened by December 2025.

source.excerpt: We trialled a new shop format – bitesize Greggs – in three high-footfall locations to test what we can achieve with a very small footprint. This agile shop format will enable us to enter more prime locations, particularly travel hubs, many of which were previously out of reach due to the size needed to run a traditional Greggs shop format. The first bitesize shop opened in Sevenoaks railway station in November. The unit is open seven days a week, creating nine new jobs for the local area

business_facts.statement: Greggs refitted 116 company-managed shops and relocated 50 shops to better serve existing customer base, with large refits including Birmingham Bull Ring, Sheffield Meadowhall and Glasgow Buchanan bus station.

source.excerpt: We opened nine new drive-thrus, six new supermarket shops and seven Outlet shops. We also expanded our presence in UK transport hubs, opening shops at Manchester Airport, and in railway stations in Leeds, St Pancras, Dartford and Sevenoaks. We moved 50 shops to new locations which allowed us to better serve our existing customer base. These included London Ludgate Hill, Cardiff St Davids, Wandsworth Southside Shopping Centre, Aberdeen Union Square and Bridgend McArthur Glen. We continued our programme of refits, refreshing and upgrading 116 company-managed shops. Larger refits include the Birmingham Bull Ring, Sheffield Meadowhall and Glasgow Buchanan bus station.

business_facts.statement: Greggs offers home delivery from more than 1,600 shops through Just Eat and Uber Eats partnerships, with delivery representing 6.8% of company-managed sales in 2025. Delivery basket value is approximately three times that of walk-in customers.

source.excerpt: We are now offering home delivery from more than 1,600 Greggs shops around the country through our partnerships with Just Eat and Uber Eats. The basket value of a delivery customer is around three times that of a walk-in customer helping to drive sales growth, with delivery now representing 6.8% of company-managed sales (2024: 6.7%).

business_facts.statement: The Greggs App was used in 26.7% of all company-managed shop transactions in 2025, up from 20.1% in 2024. The app provides loyalty rewards with free products for every nine purchases and includes gamification features via Greggs Quests.

source.excerpt: In 2025, 26.7% of all company-managed shop transactions were accompanied by an App scan (2024: 20.1%). The App is our key opportunity for rewarding customer loyalty, giving people a free product for every nine they buy. We also launched Greggs Quests to gamify the user experience and provide more opportunity to win free Greggs and other exciting prizes.

strategic_items.description: Greggs expects to open approximately 120 net new shops in 2026, with around one-third expected from franchise partners, including drive-thrus, petrol forecourts, roadsides, travel hubs, retail parks and supermarket locations.

source.excerpt: The pipeline of new shop opportunities remains strong, and we expect to open in the region of 120 net new shops in 2026, including drive-thrus, petrol forecourts, roadsides, travel hubs, retail parks and supermarket locations. We estimate that around a third of these will be with our franchise partners.

strategic_items.description: Greggs will focus on delivering excellent retail execution through improved technology and simplification of shop operations, including putting all digital orders through a single screen and standardising processes.

source.excerpt: Our key focus will be on excellent retail execution through improved technology. By giving our people the right tools to make their jobs easier, we can free them up to concentrate on delivering excellent service. We plan to simplify shop operations by putting all digital orders through a single screen, standardising our processes and freeing up space in our digital prep areas.

strategic_items.description: Greggs plans to target approximately 35 relocations and 85 refits in 2026, with focus on providing bigger and better shops to serve all channels.

source.excerpt: We will also focus on providing bigger and better shops to serve all channels by targeting around 35 relocations and 85 refits.

strategic_items.description: Greggs plans to continue exploring further growth of its home delivery service through partnerships to increase order volume from existing sites and add new shops.

source.excerpt: We will continue to explore the potential for further growth of our home delivery service, working with our partners to increase order volume from existing sites, and adding new shops.

management_commentary.statement: When launching the current strategy in 2021, Greggs set an ambition of more than 3,000 shops, which has now grown and the company is readying the business for more than 3,500 shops in the longer term.

source.excerpt: When we launched our current strategy in 2021, we set an ambition to have more than 3,000 shops. Just a few years later, our ambition has grown, and we are now readying the business for more than 3,500 shops in the longer term.

management_commentary.statement: Record numbers of customers are choosing delivery, downloading the Greggs App, and using Click + Collect to skip queues, with the app and digital channels becoming increasingly central to the business.

source.excerpt: Our digital presence continues to go from strength to strength with record numbers of customers choosing a delivery, downloading the Greggs App, and skipping the queues in-shop by using Click + Collect.

business_facts.statement: Greggs increased its share of visits in the evening (post-5pm) time slot to 2.9% in 2025, up from 2.4% in 2024

source.excerpt: In 2025, Greggs increased its share of visits across all dayparts, including the important evening (post-5pm) time slot, where share of visits increased to 2.9% (2024: 2.4%)

business_facts.statement: Evening sales represented 9.4% of company-managed food sales in 2025, up from 9.0% in 2024

source.excerpt: Evening sales as a percentage of company-managed food sales 9.4% (2024: 9.0%)

business_facts.statement: Almost 2,000 Greggs shops stay open after 5pm, with evening being the fastest growing daypart

source.excerpt: With almost 2,000 Greggs shops staying open after 5pm, the evening remains our fastest growing daypart

business_facts.statement: Mac & Cheese was successfully trialed and launched in early 2025 and is now available in over 1,830 shops

source.excerpt: Following a successful trial, we launched Mac & Cheese in early 2025, and it is now available in over 1,830 shops

business_facts.statement: Pizza Box sales occur 19% in the evening, with more than 70% of total Pizza Box sales via Uber Eats or Just Eat

source.excerpt: 19% of sales occurring in the evening. We offer pizza slices in boxes of two, four or six slices and our customers can choose from nine different flavours. We have run successful promotional bundle deals on our delivery platforms and more than 70% of our total Pizza Box sales are now via Uber Eats or Just Eat

business_facts.statement: More than 1,300 shops offer delivery service after 5pm in 2025, up from 1,200 shops in 2024

source.excerpt: We now have more than 1,300 shops offering a delivery service after 5pm (2024: 1,200)

business_facts.statement: Additional production capacity at Balliol Park now enables production of around four million more bakes per week

source.excerpt: The new equipment gives us the ability to produce around four million more bakes per week

business_facts.statement: 28% of fleet fuel usage has been switched to renewable biofuel HVO from fossil diesel in 2025, up from 13% in 2024

source.excerpt: 28% (2024: 13%) Of fleet now powered with a renewable biofuel, HVO

business_facts.statement: A new 23-acre manufacturing and logistics facility in Derby is nearing completion and scheduled to begin operating from mid-2026, with automated warehouse system for frozen goods

source.excerpt: Building work at our new 23-acre manufacturing and logistics facility in Derby is nearing completion, ready to begin operating from mid-2026. As well as providing extra capacity for Yum Yum production, it will have an automated warehouse system for frozen goods allowing us to use robotic picking

business_facts.statement: New National Distribution Centre in Kettering is being fitted out ready for launch in first quarter of 2027, with labour planning and recruitment of key staff underway

source.excerpt: We have now taken control of our new National Distribution Centre in Kettering and are fitting it out ready for its launch in first quarter of 2027. We have begun labour planning and the recruitment of key individuals who will run the new site

business_facts.statement: Migration to SAP ERP software (S/4HANA) is underway, with finance and procurement teams now using the new system; intensive communications and training programme launched in final quarter of 2025

source.excerpt: The migration to SAP has been central to this and we are now midway through moving our Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software to SAP S/4HANA. Our finance and procurement teams are now using the new system. In the final quarter of 2025, we began an intensive communications and training programme
TOTAL SALES GROWTH 6.8% 2025 6.8%
LIKE-FOR-LIKE SALES GROWTH 2.4% 2025 2.4%

financial_figures.label: Profit Before Tax (underlying, excluding exceptional items)

source.excerpt: PROFIT BEFORE TAX (£M) 171.9m 2025 £171.9

financial_figures.label: Diluted Earnings Per Share (underlying, excluding exceptional items)

source.excerpt: DILUTED EARNINGS PER SHARE (PENCE) 122.8p 2025 122.8p

financial_figures.label: Diluted Earnings Per Share (including exceptional items)
Net Cash Inflow from Operating Activities After Lease Payments

source.excerpt: NET CASH INFLOW FROM OPERATING ACTIVITIES AFTER LEASE PAYMENTS (£M) £273.7m 2025 £273.7
RETURN ON CAPITAL EMPLOYED (ROCE) 16.0% 2025 16.0%
CAPITAL EXPENDITURE (£M) £287.5m 2025 £287.5

business_facts.statement: Greggs is a retail company with company-managed shops and operates as a publicly listed plc (Greggs plc).

source.excerpt: Compares year-on-year cash sales in our company-managed shops, with more than one calendar year's trading history.

strategic_items.description: The company uses eight key financial performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor Group performance against its strategy, including total sales growth, like-for-like sales growth, profit before tax, diluted earnings per share, net cash inflow from operating activities, ROCE, capital expenditure, and liquidity.

source.excerpt: We use eight key financial performance indicators (KPIs) to monitor the performance of the Group against our strategy.

management_commentary.statement: Return on Capital Employed is a measure of the return generated on capital invested by the Group and provides a guide to how efficiently the business is generating profit with the assets used.

source.excerpt: This is a measure of the return generated on capital invested by the Group and provides a guide to how efficiently we are generating profit with the assets used in the business.

management_commentary.statement: Net cash inflow from operating activities after lease payments represents cash flows that could be used for distribution of dividends or to fund strategic objectives and is reflective of the strong cash-generative nature of the business.

source.excerpt: This represents cash flows that could be used for distribution of dividends or to fund our strategic objectives and is reflective of the strong cash-generative nature of the business.
Underlying operating profit (profit before net finance charges, exceptional items and tax) was £187.5 million in 2025
Underlying operating profit margin was 8.7% in 2025 (2024: 9.7%).
After exceptional items profit before taxation was £167.4 million in 2025
Profit after tax 122.2 153.4 -20.3%
Underlying diluted earnings per share 122.8p 137.5p -10.7%

financial_figures.label: Diluted earnings per share including exceptional items

source.excerpt: Including exceptional items diluted earnings per share were 119.3 pence (2024: 149.6 pence per share including net exceptional income).
Company-managed like-for-like sales grew by 2.4% in the year
like-for-like 'system sales' in franchised units rose by 4.3%.
Underlying return on capital employed 16.0% 20.3%
in 2025 this delivered £13.0 million of savings (2024: £10.6 million).
The business experienced overall like-for-like cost inflation of around 5.5% in 2025.
Finance income 1.8 8.1 -77.8%
Finance expense (17.4) (13.6) +27.9%
We ended the year with Group net current liabilities of £151.8 million (2024: £67.3 million)
We invested a total of £287.5 million in capital expenditure during 2025 (2024: £249.0 million).

financial_figures.label: Depreciation and amortisation on property, plant and equipment and intangibles

source.excerpt: Depreciation and amortisation on property, plant and equipment and intangibles in the year was £95.4 million (2024: £80.8 million).
A further £65.2 million (2024: £59.2 million) of depreciation was charged in respect of right-of-use assets on capitalised leases.
the interim dividend of 19.0 pence per share (2024: 19.0 pence per share) paid in October 2025
The Board recommends a final ordinary dividend of 50.0 pence per share (2024: 50.0 pence per share).
this makes a total ordinary dividend for the year of 69.0 pence per share (2024: 69.0 pence per share).

financial_figures.label: Diluted operating cash inflow per share

source.excerpt: Diluted operating cash inflow per share grew by 4.6% in 2025 to 267.1 pence (2024: 255.4 pence).
the underlying effective rate for the year was 26.8% (2024: 25.7%).
we expect capital expenditure in 2026 to be around £200 million in line with previous guidance.
we will invest in circa 135 new company-managed shops in 2026
refurbish around 45 existing company-managed shops.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates through company-managed shops, franchise partners, and business-to-business channels to franchise and grocery retail partners. The company has vertical integration in manufacturing and logistics operations.

source.excerpt: Total Group revenue reflects sales from company-managed shops, which include delivery sales, and sales through the business-to-business channel to our franchise and grocery retail partners.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates both company-managed shops and franchised units, with franchised operations primarily focused on roadside locations that have proven more resilient to market conditions.

source.excerpt: The performance of shops managed by franchise partners proved more resilient to market conditions, being primarily focused on roadside locations.

business_facts.statement: The company is investing in new distribution facilities in Derby and Kettering that will provide logistics capacity to support a network of 3,500 shops in the medium term, with flexibility to extend further if appropriate.

source.excerpt: When complete, we will have the logistics capacity to support a network of 3,500 shops in the medium term and the flexibility to extend this further if appropriate.

business_facts.statement: Greggs offers prices comparable to the grocery sector with freshly prepared food and drink offerings prepared in shops each day, and maintains a strong discount position relative to other food-to-go specialists.

source.excerpt: Our prices are comparable to the grocery sector, however, our food and drink offering is freshly prepared in shops each day.

business_facts.statement: The company targets a 25% cash return on investment for new shops and typically exceeds this level after two to three years as shops mature, with acquisition strategy targeting shops with higher than average sales and returns in new catchments.

source.excerpt: In our retail estate we continue to target a 25% cash return on investment on new shops and typically exceed this level after two to three years as shops mature.

risk_disclosures.description: Challenging market conditions in 2025 impacted trading, with subdued consumer confidence and particularly prolonged high temperatures in June and July materially impacting footfall and consumer behaviour.

source.excerpt: Subdued consumer confidence impacted trading but the Company's growth strategy remains intact

risk_disclosures.description: The business experienced overall like-for-like cost inflation of around 5.5% in 2025, primarily driven by employment costs including the impact of increased employer's National Insurance contributions from April 2025, and rising costs of food and packaging.

source.excerpt: The business experienced overall like-for-like cost inflation of around 5.5% in 2025. This was primarily driven by employment costs, including the impact of the increase in employer's National Insurance contributions from April 2025, and rising costs of food and packaging.

risk_disclosures.description: The development and commissioning of new distribution facilities in Derby and Kettering will bring additional operating and financing costs in the short term, impacting near-term margins. ROCE declined to 16.0% in 2025 from 20.3% in 2024 due to current operating conditions and increased capital employed.

source.excerpt: As we have previously guided, the development and commissioning of these sites will bring additional operating and financing costs in the short term, which will subsequently be leveraged as the new facilities allow us to open further profitable new shops.

strategic_items.description: The company is developing further partnerships to improve brand access and continuing to progress in evening daypart and delivery channels as part of its growth strategy to develop additional income streams.

source.excerpt: Greggs delivered further sales growth through new shop openings, the development of further partnerships that improve access to the brand and continued progress in the evening daypart and delivery channel.

strategic_items.description: The company is investing in logistics infrastructure and new distribution facilities in Derby and Kettering to support medium-term growth to 3,500 shops. Capital expenditure will reduce to £200m in 2026 and £150-170m from 2027 onwards, enabling material cash returns whilst pursuing disciplined capital allocation.

source.excerpt: 2025 was the peak year of our capital investment programme and, as previously communicated, expenditure will reduce materially from this point.

strategic_items.description: Management targets a ROCE of around 20% and expects it to stabilise in 2027 before recovering from 2028 onwards through shop openings in attractive locations, relocation of traditional shops to stronger locations, disciplined capital allocation, structural cost efficiency improvements, and wider market performance.

source.excerpt: Greggs targets a ROCE of around 20% and this remains one of our key objectives.

strategic_items.description: The company has a rolling programme of cost-saving initiatives leveraging vertical integration benefits and end-to-end process reviews to mitigate cost pressure, delivering £13.0 million of savings in 2025.

source.excerpt: We have a rolling programme of cost-saving initiatives with the aim of mitigating as much cost pressure as possible and in 2025 this delivered £13.0 million of savings

strategic_items.description: The Board recommends maintaining the ordinary dividend at 69.0 pence per share through the investment phase, with a progressive dividend policy aiming to increase dividends in line with growth in underlying earnings per share once returns normalise.

source.excerpt: The Board recommends maintaining the ordinary dividend through this investment phase, before returning to an ordinary dividend that is covered two times by underlying diluted earnings per share.

management_commentary.statement: Despite challenging market conditions in 2025, the Company's growth strategy remains intact, with work progressing to develop additional income streams and accelerate cost efficiencies. Along with leveraging new logistics capacity, this will support the medium-term plan to restore returns in line with historic targets.

source.excerpt: Despite challenging market conditions in 2025, Greggs delivered further sales growth through new shop openings

management_commentary.statement: Management expects like-for-like costs to be less inflationary in 2026 with overall input cost inflation of around 3%, with employment cost inflation being the biggest driver but at lower levels than recent years reflecting changes to the National Living Wage.

source.excerpt: Looking forward we expect like-for-like costs to be less inflationary in 2026, with overall input cost inflation of around 3%.

management_commentary.statement: The company currently has good levels of forward cover for commodity costs, with 100% of electricity requirements fixed for the year and forward purchase agreements in place representing approximately four months of food and packaging needs.

source.excerpt: We currently have good levels of forward cover for commodity costs, with 100% of our electricity requirements fixed for the year

management_commentary.statement: Management believes Greggs is well positioned to weather short-term market pressures whilst benefiting as the consumer environment improves. ROCE is expected to reduce further in 2026 as new distribution facilities are brought into use, stabilise in 2027, and recover from 2028 onwards.

source.excerpt: we believe that Greggs is well placed to weather the short-term pressures

management_commentary.statement: Management expects the effective tax rate for 2026 to be around 26.0% and going forward the rate is expected to remain around one percentage point above the headline corporation tax rate, principally due to expenditure for which no tax relief is available.

source.excerpt: We expect the effective tax rate for 2026 to be around 26.0%

financial_figures.label: Net cash inflow from operating activities after lease payments

source.excerpt: The net cash inflow from operating activities after lease payments in the year was £273.7 million (2024: £261.9 million).
At the end of the year the Group had net cash and cash equivalents of £45.8 million (2024: £125.3 million)
representing £70.8 million of cash and cash equivalents, offset by £25 million drawn on the Company's RCF.
representing £70.8 million of cash and cash equivalents, offset by £25 million drawn on the Company's RCF.
The facility provides liquidity of £100 million in committed funds.
total available liquidity at the end of 2025 was £145.8 million (2024: £225.3 million).
the scheme is in a net liability position of £0.3 million (2024: £0.4 million net liability)

business_facts.statement: Greggs is investing in significant new sites in Derby and Kettering to expand its supply chain and support profitable growth.

source.excerpt: The significant investments we are making to support further profitable growth create short-term ROCE and margin headwinds as we bring important new sites into our supply chain in a period where underlying trading has seen pressure from market operating conditions.

business_facts.statement: Greggs targets maintenance capital expenditure at around 5% of revenue in normal circumstances, which will reduce following the significant investment in new sites in Derby and Kettering.

source.excerpt: In normal circumstances we expect maintenance capital expenditure to be around 5% of revenue. The level of maintenance capital expenditure will reduce following the significant investment in the new sites in Derby and Kettering.

business_facts.statement: Greggs targets a progressive ordinary dividend normally covered around two times by underlying profit after taxation.

source.excerpt: We continue to target a progressive ordinary dividend, normally around two times covered by underlying profit after taxation.

risk_disclosures.description: Internal business interruption risks have decreased as mitigating actions have reduced the impact of any such issues, though cyber risk in the market has increased and the company continues to take significant steps to improve controls and strengthen resilience.

source.excerpt: The risk relating to internal business interruption has decreased, as we consider the impact of any such issue has been reduced by our mitigating actions. We recognise that the overall level of cyber risk in the market has increased, although we continue to take significant steps to improve our controls and strengthen our resilience.

risk_disclosures.description: A principal risk relating to access to liquidity has been included, in the context of a material reduction in solvency without prior warning, rather than a gradual decline over time.

source.excerpt: we have reflected on our principal risk disclosure, and agreed that we should include a financial risk relating to access to liquidity. This would be in the context of a material reduction in solvency without prior warning, rather than a gradual decline over a period of time.

risk_disclosures.description: Climate-related risks are integrated within the risk management process and strategic and functional registers. The company does not currently consider failure to effectively respond to climate-related impacts as a principal risk within the time horizon of current plans.

source.excerpt: Our climate-related risks are integrated within our risk management process and are captured within our strategic and functional registers. We remain of the view that our strategic risk of 'a failure to effectively respond to climate-related impacts on our business' does not constitute a principal risk within the time horizon of our current plans.

strategic_items.description: Return surplus cash to shareholders where net cash exceeds minimum requirement, taking into account reserves for growth investments, through special dividends or share buybacks.

source.excerpt: Return surplus cash to shareholders. Where net cash on the balance sheet exceeds our minimum requirement, taking into account that reserved for growth investments, we expect to return cash to shareholders by way of either special dividends or share buybacks.

strategic_items.description: Selective investment in opportunities that deliver attractive returns, including expansion of the estate and support for generation of additional income streams.

source.excerpt: Selectively invest to grow. We will continue to invest in opportunities that deliver attractive returns, including the expansion of our estate and to support the generation of additional income streams where relevant.

strategic_items.description: Material controls and contingency plans for business-critical activities have been a key focus, with continued development of the risk management framework to ensure risks are identified and appropriately managed.

source.excerpt: The focus of meetings outside standing agenda items has been on material controls and contingency plans for business-critical activities.

management_commentary.statement: The significant investments to support profitable growth create short-term ROCE and margin headwinds as new sites are brought into the supply chain during a period of pressure from market operating conditions.

source.excerpt: The significant investments we are making to support further profitable growth create short-term ROCE and margin headwinds as we bring important new sites into our supply chain in a period where underlying trading has seen pressure from market operating conditions.

management_commentary.statement: Investment in additional capacity will enable Greggs to realise the medium-term opportunity to grow its estate and expand into new channels, while progressing opportunities to develop additional income streams and accelerate structural cost savings.

source.excerpt: Our investment in additional capacity will enable Greggs to realise the medium-term opportunity to grow its estate and expand into new channels, whilst also progressing opportunities to develop additional income streams and accelerate structural cost savings.

management_commentary.statement: Greggs aims to maintain a year-end net cash position of circa 3% of revenue in normal circumstances to allow for seasonality in the working capital cycle and to protect creditor interests.

source.excerpt: we aim, in normal circumstances, to maintain a year-end net cash position of circa 3% of revenue in order to allow for seasonality in the working capital cycle and to protect the interests of all creditors.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates a diversified product range and franchise partnership model alongside grocery retail sales and delivery channels.

source.excerpt: This would primarily impact our own customers, including those of our franchise partners, but also potentially our grocery retail sales.

business_facts.statement: Greggs avoids single source supply for key ingredients with risk mitigation plans in place where necessary.

source.excerpt: We avoid single source supply for key ingredients as far as possible, with risk mitigations plans in place where necessary.

business_facts.statement: Greggs operates through company-owned stores, franchise partners, and grocery retail channels.

source.excerpt: Our strategy and goals may not be fully aligned with those of our partners in franchise, grocery retail or delivery.

risk_disclosures.description: Potential loss of capacity at a key production facility could prevent supply of full product range to customers, franchise partners and grocery retail sales for an extended period.

source.excerpt: We could suffer a significant reduction in product availability as a result of the total loss of capacity at a key production facility.

risk_disclosures.description: Prolonged outage or significant issue at a key supplier or within their supply chain could impact ability to produce parts of product range, due to external business interruption, geopolitical instability, or food safety concerns.

source.excerpt: Supply from a key third party could be interrupted. This could be a result of issues such as external business interruption, geopolitical instability, or a food safety concern.

risk_disclosures.description: Cyber incident or data breach could result in significant loss of data, litigation, fines, and data unavailability impacting ability to operate.

source.excerpt: We could suffer a significant loss of data, resulting in litigation and fines. Data may be unavailable or lost, making it difficult for us to operate.

risk_disclosures.description: IT system interruptions and cyber incidents could cause extended business disruption, as operations and business-critical activities rely on technology.

source.excerpt: Our reliance on technology means that system interruptions and cyber incidents are potentially more disruptive, with a more significant impact on business operations.

risk_disclosures.description: Misalignment with franchise, grocery retail or delivery partners could limit service in desired locations, affect performance and damage brand reputation.

source.excerpt: This would limit our ability to offer our service in locations where our customers want us to be.

risk_disclosures.description: Inability to attract, retain and motivate the right talent could prevent delivery of existing product range and service standards, increase staff turnover and training burden, and limit business growth.

source.excerpt: We may be unable to attract and retain the right talent within Greggs to maintain our culture and operate as our customers expect.

risk_disclosures.description: Greater risk of brand reputation damage by internal or external sources as brand profile grows, which could cause customers to lose trust and reduce shareholder value.

source.excerpt: There is greater risk of damage to our brand reputation by internal or external sources as our brand profile grows.

risk_disclosures.description: Potential to produce/sell unsafe products or inappropriate quality due to incorrect allergen labelling, product contamination or procedure non-compliance, risking customer/colleague harm, brand damage, sales impact and significant fines.

source.excerpt: We may produce and/or sell products which are unsafe, or not of the appropriate quality. This could be a result of incorrect labelling of allergens, product contamination, or a failure to comply with procedures.

risk_disclosures.description: New regulatory requirements driven by environmental, health or other concerns could require product range changes or advertising approach modifications, creating risk of market share loss or regulatory action if unable to respond quickly.

source.excerpt: New regulatory requirements could be implemented, driven by environmental, health or other concerns.

risk_disclosures.description: Business may not be able to access required liquidity facilities to deliver on plans, potentially requiring delays to investment plans to prioritise financial commitments to employees, suppliers and property providers.

source.excerpt: The business may not be able to access the liquidity facilities required to deliver on its plans.

strategic_items.description: Five strategic pillars guide Greggs' strategy: Great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink; Best customer experience; Competitive supply chain; First-class support teams; and The Greggs Pledge.

source.excerpt: STRATEGIC PILLARS 1 Great tasting, freshly prepared food and drink 2 Best customer experience 3 Competitive supply chain 4 First-class support teams 5 The Greggs Pledge

strategic_items.description: Greggs is rolling out a standardised Business Continuity Management approach across supply sites with contingency plans that are reviewed and tested periodically.

source.excerpt: We are continuing to roll out a standardised Business Continuity Management approach across our supply sites.

strategic_items.description: Greggs is moving towards more cloud-based solutions across operations to increase resilience within the network and is developing business continuity and disaster recovery capability.

source.excerpt: We continue to move towards more cloud-based solutions across our operations, which increases resilience within our network.

strategic_items.description: A committed RCF (Revolving Credit Facility) is in place with significant undrawn capacity, and Greggs has discretion over uncommitted investment plans to manage liquidity.

source.excerpt: A committed RCF is in place, with significant undrawn capacity.

business_facts.statement: Greggs has vertical integration in its supply chain, meaning the cost of the internal supply chain cannot be reduced quickly in a significant loss of sales scenario.

source.excerpt: A significant loss of sales is particularly damaging given the Group's vertical integration in that the cost of the internal supply chain cannot be reduced quickly.

business_facts.statement: The Group has grocery retail relationships with Iceland Foods and Tesco for delivery sales and 'Bake at Home' products.

source.excerpt: Delivery sales and 'Bake at Home' sales through the Group's grocery retail relationships with Iceland Foods and Tesco are assumed to be most resilient.

business_facts.statement: The Group has a savoury products manufacturing facility at Balliol Park.

source.excerpt: Operational disruption impacting product availability, resulting from either the loss of supply of savoury products from Balliol Park or loss of key IT systems.

risk_disclosures.description: Loss of supply of savoury products from Balliol Park or loss of key IT systems could result in operational disruption and product availability impacts.

source.excerpt: Operational disruption impacting product availability, resulting from either the loss of supply of savoury products from Balliol Park or loss of key IT systems.

risk_disclosures.description: A brand-damaging food scare could result in a significant circa 25% sales reduction for the initial six months followed by gradual recovery of confidence.

source.excerpt: A brand-damaging food scare resulting in a significant one-year sales reduction (circa 25% sales reduction for initial six months) followed by gradual recovery of confidence.

risk_disclosures.description: A pandemic threat could result in significantly subdued trade due to lockdown rules, with an assumed circa 30% reduction in overall Group sales through a three-month lockdown in the final quarter of 2026, with phased recovery through 2027.

source.excerpt: Pandemic threat – the risk that trade is significantly subdued as a result of lockdown rules, with the Group continuing to trade as an essential retailer selling food, which was permitted during the Covid-19 pandemic. This subdues walk-in trade through the lockdown period and as the economy recovers (a circa 30% reduction in overall Group sales through a three-month lockdown in the final quarter of 2026, with a phased recovery through 2027).

risk_disclosures.description: Stress testing under multiple principal risk scenarios showed a potential breach of loan covenants in 2027, although the Directors consider the likelihood of this to be remote.

source.excerpt: Although the Group has sufficient existing and committed financing facilities to manage in a situation where multiple principal risk scenarios occur concurrently, the simulation showed a breach of loan covenants in 2027, but the directors consider the likelihood of this to be remote.

management_commentary.statement: The Directors have a reasonable expectation that the Group will be able to continue in operation and meet its liabilities as they fall due over the three-year period of their detailed assessment to 2028.

source.excerpt: Based on the results of the analysis, the Directors have a reasonable expectation that the Group will be able to continue in operation and meet its liabilities as they fall due over the three-year period of their detailed assessment.

management_commentary.statement: The Group has sufficient existing and committed financing facilities to manage in a situation where multiple principal risk scenarios occur concurrently, and Directors believe the Group's borrowing capacity would be sufficient to allow access to temporary additional facilities if needed.

source.excerpt: The Group has sufficient existing and committed financing facilities to manage in a situation where multiple principal risk scenarios occur concurrently. If this were not the case, the Directors believe that the borrowing capacity of the Group would be sufficient to allow it access to temporary additional facilities.

business_facts.statement: Greggs is a company incorporated and domiciled in the UK with company-managed shops and business-to-business operations

source.excerpt: Greggs plc (the 'Company') is a company incorporated and domiciled in the UK. The Group accounts consolidate those of the Company and its subsidiaries (together referred to as the 'Group')

business_facts.statement: Volumes have been under pressure from reduced consumer spending impacting the wider food-to-go market

source.excerpt: volumes have been under pressure from reduced consumer spending impacting the wider food-to-go market

business_facts.statement: The Group operates property leases that typically run for a period of 10 or 15 years, with the majority in England and Wales protected by the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954.

source.excerpt: The leases typically run for a period of 10 or 15 years. In England and Wales, the majority of the Group's property leases are protected by the Landlord and Tenant Act 1954 (LTA) which affords protection to the lessee at the end of an existing lease term.

business_facts.statement: Some property leases contain variable payment terms linked to sales generated from individual shops, with up to 100% of lease payments on a basis of variable payment terms for some locations.

source.excerpt: Some property leases contain variable payment terms that are linked to sales generated from a shop. For individual shops, up to 100% of lease payments are on the basis of variable payment terms.

business_facts.statement: The Group has a growing business-to-business segment and is exploring other growth opportunities in addition to company-managed estate expansion.

source.excerpt: a growing business-to-business segment, overheads incurred in respect of growing the company-managed estate and overheads related to exploring other growth opportunities.
